How does authentication work in Foundry: Authentication in Foundry IQ uses Azure Active Directory with the DefaultAzureCredential chain. The credential tries managed identity first (for Azure-hosted services), then falls back to CLI credentials (az login), Visual Studio Code credentials, and environment variables. Tokens expire after 60 minutes and are automatically refreshed. Required roles: Foundry IQ Reader for search, Contributor for writes, Admin for schema changes.
What is a knowledge base in Foundry IQ?: A knowledge base (KB) in Foundry IQ is a structured collection of documents with semantic search capabilities. Each KB belongs to a project and has a configured embedding model (typically text-embedding-3-small), chunk size, and access control policies. Documents are the primary storage unit, each with content, metadata, and an auto-computed embedding vector. Multiple agents can share a single KB by connecting with the same project and KB name.
How do I search a knowledge base?: Search a Foundry IQ knowledge base using POST /v1/projects/{project}/kbs/{kb}/search with a JSON body containing 'query' (the search text), 'k' (max results), and optional 'filter' (metadata conditions). The API returns ranked documents with relevance scores. Foundry IQ handles embedding the query and computing similarity internally.
How do I write documents to a knowledge: Write documents to Foundry IQ using POST /v1/projects/{project}/kbs/{kb}/documents with a JSON body containing 'id' (optional), 'content' (text to embed), and 'metadata' (arbitrary JSON). The embedding vector is computed automatically. Use PUT for updates and DELETE for removal. Batch writes are supported for bulk operations.
What are the best practices for agent me: Agent memory best practices: 1) Store Q&A pairs as atoms with full provenance. 2) Use promotion to consolidate repeated observations into patterns. 3) Apply exponential decay so stale memories fade from search. 4) Actively heal contradictions rather than silently ignoring them. 5) Track source documents for freshness checking. 6) Make memory a tool the agent calls, not a prefix injection. 7) Cross-agent inheritance should be visible and attributed.
